---
title: "R Notebook"
output:
  pdf_document: default
  html_notebook: default
---

# Linear Regression

Kan två (eller fler) numeriska mätvärden beskrivas av ett linjärt samband kx+m?

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
londHus <- read.csv("london_houses.csv")
prisYta <- lm(Price.... ~ Square.Meters, data = londHus)
summary(prisYta)
```

```{r}
plot(londHus$Price.... ~ londHus$Square.Meters)
abline(prisYta, col = "red")
```

```{r}

# Group by a categorical variable (e.g., "Neighborhood") and fit linear models
grouped_models <- by(londHus, londHus$Neighborhood, function(df) {
  lm(Price.... ~ Square.Meters, data = df)
})

# View the results for each group
lapply(grouped_models, summary)

```

# Andra tester

```{r}
kruskal.test(Price.... ~ Neighborhood, data = londHus)

wilcox.test(Price.... ~ Garden, data = londHus)
```

# Flera dimensioner

## chi2-test

```{r}
gard_heat_balc <- with(londHus, table(Garden, Heating.Type, Balcony))

# gard_heat_balc

summary(gard_heat_balc)
```

## Anova

```{r}
# Two-way ANOVA with two categorical factors (garden and balcony) and one numerical variable (price)
anova_result <- aov(Price.... ~ Garden * Balcony, data = londHus)

# Summary of the ANOVA result
summary(anova_result)
```

# GGPlot2

```{r}
ggplot(londHus, aes(x = Price....)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 200000, fill = "blue", color = "black") +
  labs(title = "Histogram of House Prices", x = "Price (£)", y = "Frequency")
```

```{r}
# Simple boxplot for Price across different Property Types
ggplot(londHus, aes(x = Property.Type, y = Price....)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "lightblue", color = "darkblue") +
  labs(title = "Price Distribution by Property Type", x = "Property Type", y = "Price (£)")
```

```{r}
# Boxplot comparing Price between Garden and Property Type
ggplot(londHus, aes(x = Property.Type, y = Price...., fill = Garden)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Price by Property Type and Garden", x = "Property Type", y = "Price (£)")
```

```{r}
# Boxplot comparing Price between Garden and Property Type
ggplot(londHus, aes(x = Property.Type, y = Price...., fill = interaction(Garden, Balcony))) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Price by Property Type and Garden", x = "Property Type", y = "Price (£)")
```

```{r}
# Scatter plot with two factors (color and shape) and a continuous variable (Price)
ggplot(londHus, aes(y = Price...., x = Square.Meters, color = Balcony, shape = Property.Type)) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title = "Scatter Plot of Price vs Size, Colored by Balcony", y = "Price (£)", x = "Size (sq ft)")
```

```{r}
# Scatter plot of Price vs. Size, with facets for Property Type and Garden
ggplot(londHus, aes(x = Square.Meters, y = Price....)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = Property.Type)) +
  facet_grid(Garden ~ Property.Type) +
  labs(title = "Price vs Size by Property Type and Garden", x = "Size (sq ft)", y = "Price (£)")
```

# Lite detektivarbete

```{r}
chelseaPrice <- subset(londHus, Neighborhood == "Chelsea")
greenwichPrice <- subset(londHus, Neighborhood == "Greenwich")

chelseaModel <- lm(chelseaPrice$Price.... ~ chelseaPrice$Square.Meters)
gwModel <- lm(greenwichPrice$Price.... ~ greenwichPrice$Square.Meters)

summary(chelseaModel)
summary(gwModel)

```

```{r}
# Filter data for Chelsea and Greenwich
chelsea_data <- subset(londHus, Neighborhood == "Chelsea")
greenwich_data <- subset(londHus, Neighborhood == "Greenwich")

# Set up the plot
plot(londHus$Square.Meters, londHus$Price...., type = "n", 
     xlab = "Square Meters", ylab = "Price", 
     main = "Price vs. Square Meters by Neighborhood")

# Add points and regression lines for Chelsea
points(chelsea_data$Square.Meters, chelsea_data$Price...., col = as.factor(chelsea_data$Property.Type), pch = 16)
abline(lm(Price.... ~ Square.Meters, data = chelsea_data), col = "red")

# Add points and regression lines for Greenwich
points(greenwich_data$Square.Meters, greenwich_data$Price...., col = as.factor(greenwich_data$Property.Type), pch = 16)
abline(lm(Price.... ~ Square.Meters, data = greenwich_data), col = "blue")

# Add a legend
legend("topright", legend = c("Chelsea", "Greenwich"), col = c("red", "blue"), pch = 16, title = "Neighborhood")
```

```{r}
plot(londHus$Square.Meters, londHus$Price...., 
     xlab = "Square Meters", ylab = "Price", 
     main = "Price vs. Square Meters (Colored by Property Type)", 
     pch = 16, col = as.factor(londHus$Property.Type))
```

```{r}
#lm(formula = Price.... ~ Square.Meters * Property.Type * Neighborhood, data = londhus)

summary(lm(formula = Price.... ~ Square.Meters * Property.Type, data = chelsea_data))
summary(lm(formula = Price.... ~ Square.Meters * Property.Type, data = greenwich_data))

```

```{r}
ggplot(londHus, aes(x = Square.Meters, y = Price....)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = Neighborhood)) +
  facet_grid(Neighborhood ~ Property.Type) +
  labs(title = "Price vs Size by Property Type and Garden", x = "Size (sq ft)", y = "Price (£)")
```
